name: Summarize documents with Notion and Slack on autopilot

options.systemPromptTemplate: You are a precise extraction engine. Only extract values present in the text.

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf02984-email`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Collect Raw Text

### Collect Raw Text

Shapes the item: writes `content`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Extract Structured Fields

### Extract Structured Fields

Pulls structured fields (messageId, from, subject) out of free text using a strict extraction prompt.

Next: All Required Fields Found?

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Extract Structured Fields** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### All Required Fields Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.output?.messageId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Upsert into Notion · ②→ Queue for Manual Review

### Upsert into Notion

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Next: Merge Review Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Queue for Manual Review

Posts a message to Slack (`#inbox-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Merge Review Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Merge Review Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

# Summarize documents with Notion and Slack on autopilot

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Summarize documents with Notion and Slack on autopilot. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual auto-responders is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Collect Raw Text → Extract Structured Fields → All Required Fields Found? → Upsert into Notion → Merge Review Paths

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating auto-responders in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Notion integration token; OpenRouter API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The required-fields check runs *before* the write, not after — an incomplete extraction goes to manual review instead of landing a half-populated record in the system of record. Upsert (not plain create) means re-processing the same document twice updates the existing record instead of duplicating it.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a new field to the extraction schema and mark it required — watch more documents route to manual review.
2. Feed it a document deliberately missing a required field and trace exactly where it ends up.
3. Change the extraction target (e.g. invoice → contract fields) and adjust the upsert key to match.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your auto-responders process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
